equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: the current payload builder has no RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK cap and does not skip delivery-ready collection when the global deadline has already expired.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing default-64/environment cap and deadline-expired skip for Phase C delivery settlement collection.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ethereum/payload/src/lib.rs checks cancellation inside the delivery loop and global_deadline later before ordering/execution, but not before collecting delivery_ready_async_entries.
crates/transaction-pool/src/pool/mod.rs exposes get_delivery_ready_original_async_transactions(current_block) without any limit parameter.
No audited repo contains RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK or MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK.

rationale: The primary diff adds the same deadline guard and environment-configurable cap to Phase C delivery settlements, preventing unbounded delivery backlog work during block building. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Payload building must skip Phase C delivery settlement collection if the global build deadline has expired, and otherwise cap delivery-ready transactions to `RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK` with a default of 64.
suggested_falsification_tests: Provide a pool with more than 64 delivery-ready transactions and assert payload building processes only 64 by default; set the deadline in the past and assert no delivery-ready transactions are collected for that block.
summary: Delivery settlements are not capped or pre-skipped by deadline in the current port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Force the builder deadline into the past before Phase C and observe that current code still calls get_delivery_ready_original_async_transactions before the later execution deadline check.
title: Delivery settlements are capped
